mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in PAYEMS indicates expanding payroll income and lower near-term recession risk, causing investors to mark up expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month PAYEMS growth is followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC below 0 over a 3-year rolling walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 1.75%, the market prices a tighter discount-rate path that lowers equity duration values and weighs on risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS crossings are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample cases.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where RSAFS rises over 3 months while HOUST falls over 3 months signals consumption strength masking weakness in rate-sensitive housing, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as demand breadth deteriorates.
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS-HOUST divergence has nonnegative average 60-day forward excess returns or Rank IC above -0.01 fails to appear in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in INDPRO signals stronger production volumes and operating leverage, raising expected near-term earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO 3-month trends do not deliver higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative trends at p <= 0.10 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, labor-market slack is increasing fast enough to signal profit-cycle deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or average returns are not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is rising while UNRATE is below 4.2%, the central bank is tightening into a tight labor market, increasing discount rates before unemployment relief appears and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if this joint condition does not produce lower 60-day returns than periods with either condition alone in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 and RSAFS both rise on their latest available growth windows, aggregate demand is broad enough to support revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly change > 0 and RSAFS 3-month change > 0
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDPC1 and RSAFS growth fails to produce positive mean 60-day returns or has Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation momentum accelerates above its prior 6-month pace, investors price higher policy-rate risk and margin pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change
failure_condition: Reject if CPI acceleration triggers are followed by nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or the short-horizon hit rate is not below 50%.
